explanation: The property states that globally, whenever there is any change in alarm settings, the system must log those alarm settings. This directly translates the requirement that the system 'shall provide a log to include any change of alarm settings'.

The property states that globally, whenever there is any change in alarm settings, the system must log those alarm settings. This is captured by the LTL formula G (alarmSettingsChanged -> logAlarmSettings). The natural language phrase 'including the value applied' implies that the content of the log will contain this information, which is consistent with the atomic proposition 'logAlarmSettings'. No specific duration is mentioned, so N_DURATION is null.

The property states that globally, whenever any alarm settings are changed (represented by 'alarmSettingsChanged'), the system must immediately log these alarm settings (represented by 'logAlarmSettings'). This ensures that every change to alarm settings is recorded, fulfilling the requirement to 'provide a log to include any change of alarm settings, including the value applied'.

The property states that globally, whenever any alarm settings are changed, the system logs these alarm settings. This directly captures the requirement for the system to provide a log including changes of alarm settings and their applied values.

The property states that globally, whenever any alarm settings are changed (represented by 'alarmSettingsChanged'), the system must log these alarm settings (represented by 'logAlarmSettings'). This ensures that every modification to alarm settings is recorded as required.

The property states that globally, whenever the alarm settings are changed, the system must log these alarm settings. This directly captures the requirement for logging any change in alarm settings, including the value applied (which is inherent in the 'log alarm settings' proposition).
The property states that whenever 'alarmSettingsChanged' occurs, 'logAlarmSettings' must also occur. This is a common 'whenever P then Q' response pattern, best captured by the LTL formula G(P -> Q).

The property states that globally, whenever any change of alarm settings occurs, the system must log those alarm settings. This captures the requirement for a log to be provided that includes any change of alarm settings and the value applied.

This property states that globally, whenever any change of alarm settings occurs, the system must immediately log these alarm settings. This ensures that every modification to alarm settings, including the specific values applied, is consistently recorded in the log.

The property states that globally, whenever any change of alarm settings occurs (alarmSettingsChanged), a log of alarm settings is provided (logAlarmSettings). This captures the requirement that every instance of alarm setting changes must be logged, including the new values.
